cards.title: The one-sentence definition
cards.body:
An API is a remote service your software can call - with a fixed format for questions and answers - so a program can get work done without a human clicking buttons.

              Not a website. Not a chat window. A machine contract: send structured data in, get structured data out.

              
                Your codescript, bot, app

                →
                HTTP requestJSON + API key

                →
                xAI serversGrok runs here

                →
                HTTP responseJSON back

              

              
                Analogy (accurate enough)

                Restaurant kitchen. You don't walk into the kitchen. You write an order on a standard form (request). The kitchen produces food (response). The menu + order form = the API specification. The waiter = HTTP.

cards.title: API vs using Grok in a browser
cards.body:
Browser (grok.com, Grok app)API (your key + code)

                Who talks to GrokYou, typingYour program, automatically

                When it runsWhen you're there24/7 - bot, cron job, app button

                What you paySubscription (if any)Your API credits (example balance)

                Output goes toChat windowWherever your code sends it (Discord, file, speaker)

                Same brain?Same models. Different door into the building.

              
              
                When you do NOT need the API

                One-off questions, brainstorming, editing a paragraph, exploring an idea. Use the browser. Cheaper mentally, zero integration work.

              

              
                When you DO need the API

                Something must run without you: a Discord bot, an app button for a family member, batch-processing 40 story chapters, generating images from a script, voice that plays when a file lands in a folder.

cards.title: What you already did (mapped to API concepts)
cards.body:
You've touched API territory without necessarily knowing the vocabulary.

              
                
                  Cloned your voice

                  That used a Voice API capability - text in, audio out, with a voice profile. Someone else's servers rendered audio from your text. That's an API call whether you clicked a button or ran a script.

                

                
                  Built a client app for a non-technical user

                  That app is a client. Somewhere in the chain it either calls xAI directly (API key in the app) or calls your own backend that calls xAI. The user sees buttons; under the hood it's requests and responses.

                

                
                  Grok Build / Cursor

                  The IDE agent uses an API key (yours or bundled) to send your prompts to a coding model (grok-build-0.1). You're already a consumer of API infrastructure - you just didn't see the JSON.

                

              

              The bootcamp makes the hidden layer visible: what gets sent, what comes back, what it costs.

cards.title: Module 1 checklist
cards.body:
I can explain API in one sentence: program sends structured request, gets structured response

                
I know browser Grok vs API is same models, different access path

                
I can name one thing I did (voice clone, app) and which API category it used

cards.title: What you see after login
cards.body:
Screen description - match this to console.x.ai:

              
                Welcome, [your account] · xAI Console

                
                  Create API keyDone ✓

                  Enable auto top upYour choice - see Module 4

                  Credits remaining$50

                  Credits usage$0.00

                  Tokens0

                  Requests0

                

              

              Credits remaining = prepaid balance. Every API call deducts from this.

              Tokens = units of text the model reads/writes. Usage dashboard aggregates them.

              Requests = number of API calls. One Discord message = one request (roughly).

              $0.00 usage with $50 sitting there = key exists, nothing has called the API yet. Grok Build in the IDE may use separate billing - check which key powers what.

cards.title: The four products on your dashboard
cards.body:
Grok Build 0.1 New

                  Coding/agent model. Built for multi-step engineering tasks in IDEs and agents.

                  Use when: Grok Build, Cursor, automated code edits, workflow agents.
Endpoint: Same API base, model name grok-build-0.1.
Cost: ~$1.00 / 1M input tokens, $2.00 / 1M output.

                

                
                  Grok 4.3 - Chat API Core

                  Text generation, multi-turn chat, tool/function calling.

                  Use when: Bots, Q&A, story generation, summarization, anything text.
Endpoint: POST /v1/responses or chat completions.
Cost: ~$1.25 / 1M input, $2.50 / 1M output tokens.

                

                
                  Voice Agents New

                  Real-time voice: listen, think, speak. Can tie to phone numbers.

                  Use when: Phone agent, live voice conversation, duplex audio.
Cost: Realtime ~$0.05/min ($3/hr). TTS ~$15 / 1M characters.

                

                
                  Imagine API

                  Image and video generation from text (and image-to-video).

                  Use when: Thumbnails, storyboards, visual assets.
Cost: Images $0.02-0.05 each. Video $0.05-0.08 / second.

                

              

              These are not separate accounts. One credit pool. Voice marathon burns it faster than text homework.

cards.title: API key - what it is and isn't
cards.body:
Your API key is a password that programs use, not you.

              
                Format: Long string, sent as Authorization: Bearer YOUR_KEY on every request.

                Scope: Charges your team's credits. Anyone with the key can spend your $50.

                Not your login password. Not shown in chat UIs. Lives in env vars, config files, or app secrets.

                Rotation: Revoke and recreate in console if leaked (Discord, GitHub, screenshot).

              
              
                Security rule

                Never paste the key in a public repo, Discord, or a browser extension you don't trust. For learning on your own PC, local scripts are fine.

              

              Auto top-up

              When credits hit zero, requests fail. Auto top-up charges your card automatically. For learning phase: optional. You have $50; that's thousands of small text experiments. Enable when you deploy something production-grade that can't afford downtime.

cards.title: Module 2 checklist
cards.body:
I can identify credits, tokens, and requests on my console

                
I know all four products draw from the same $50 pool

                
I understand the API key is a billing password for programs

cards.title: The four parts every call has
cards.body:
PartWhat it isExample

                URL (endpoint)Address of the capabilityhttps://api.x.ai/v1/responses

                MethodWhat action (almost always POST for AI)POST

                HeadersMetadata: auth, content typeAuthorization: Bearer xai-...

                BodyThe actual order (JSON)model, messages, temperature…

              
              The response is also JSON: model output, token counts, sometimes tool results. Your job as API user: build the body, parse the response, handle errors.

cards.title: JSON - the order form
cards.body:
JSON is nested key-value text. Machines parse it. You read it with practice.

              {
  "model": "grok-4.3",
  "input": [
    { "role": "system", "content": "You answer in one sentence." },
    { "role": "user", "content": "What is an API?" }
  ]
}
              
                FieldMeaning

                modelWhich brain. Wrong model = wrong price/behavior.

                role: systemInstructions the user doesn't see. Sets behavior.

                role: userThe actual prompt.

                role: assistantPrevious model replies - for multi-turn chat history.

              
              Optional knobs: temperature (randomness), max_tokens (cap on reply length), tools array (lets model call functions).

cards.title: What comes back
cards.body:
Success looks like HTTP status 200 and a JSON body with the generated text plus billing metadata.

              {
  "id": "resp_...",
  "model": "grok-4.3",
  "output": [...],
  "usage": {
    "input_tokens": 42,
    "output_tokens": 87,
    "total_tokens": 129
  }
}
              input_tokens = what you sent (including system prompt + history).

              output_tokens = what the model wrote.

              That's how $50 becomes line items.

              
                Common errors (theory)

                
                  401 - bad or missing API key

                  402 / insufficient credits - balance empty

                  429 - too many requests too fast (rate limit)

                  400 - malformed JSON or invalid model name

cards.title: First live call (instructions)
Module 3 checklist
cards.body:
I can name URL, headers, body as the three request parts

                
I understand model, system, user roles in JSON

                
I know input_tokens and output_tokens drive cost

                
I ran the PowerShell example OR know exactly what success looks like

cards.title: How $50 actually depletes
cards.body:
Text is cheap. Voice realtime and video are not. Source: xAI pricing docs (USD).

              
                ProductUnitApprox cost

                Grok 4.3 text1M input tokens$1.25

                Grok 4.3 text1M output tokens$2.50

                Grok Build 0.11M input / output$1.00 / $2.00

                Imagine imageper image$0.02 - $0.05

                Imagine videoper second$0.05 - $0.08

                Voice realtimeper minute$0.05 ($3/hr)

                TTS (narration)1M characters$15

              
              Rule of thumb: 1 token ≈ ¾ of an English word. A 500-word reply ≈ 650 output tokens ≈ $0.0016. Pennies.

cards.title: Cost estimator
cards.body:
Adjust inputs. This is approximate - actual bills include system prompts, history, tools.

              
                
                  
                    Task type
                    
                      Text chat (Grok 4.3)
                      Coding (Grok Build)
                      Image (Imagine)
                      TTS narration (chars)
                      Voice realtime (minutes)
                    
                  

                  
                    Requests per day
                    
                  

                  
                    Words per request (in+out)
                    
                  

                  
                    Days per month
                    
                  

                  

                

              

              
                What drains $50 fast

                
                  Always-on voice agent (hours × $3/hr)

                  Long video generation (seconds × $0.05+)

                  Tool-heavy agents (web search $5 / 1k calls on top of tokens)

                  Huge context pasted every request (100k+ tokens × hundreds of calls)

cards.title: $50 learning strategy
cards.body:
Phase 1 - Learn (week 1-2): Short prompts, grok-4.3, manual tests. Expected spend: under $1.

                Phase 2 - Prototype: One bot or one narration pipeline. Cap max_tokens. Log usage daily in console.

                Phase 3 - Decide on auto top-up: Only when something must stay up and you'd rather pay than fix downtime.

              
              
                ActivityRough monthly cost

                50 short API lessons< $0.10

                Discord bot, 100 msgs/day, medium replies$2 - $15

                Narrate 2hr audiobook via TTS~$15-30 (char-based)

                Voice agent, 1 hr/day realtime~$90/mo - burns $50 in ~2 weeks

              
              Your $50 is plenty for education + several prototypes. It is not plenty for an unmanaged 24/7 voice bot.

cards.title: Module 4 checklist
cards.body:
I know text vs voice vs video cost order of magnitude

                
I used the calculator for one scenario I care about

                
I have a decision on auto top-up (on/off) with a reason

cards.title: Function / tool calling
cards.body:
The model doesn't just talk - it can request that your code run a function (look up weather, query a database, post to Discord).

              
                User asks"What's in stock?"

                →
                Modelreturns tool_call

                →
                Your coderuns function

                →
                Modelfinal answer

              

              This is how "perform functions" works in Discord bots and agents. The API conversation bounces between xAI and your handler. Extra cost: tool invocation fees (e.g. web search $5/1000 calls) plus tokens.

cards.title: Voice: three different things
cards.body:
CapabilityWhat it doesWhen to use

                TTS (text-to-speech)Text → audio file/streamNarration, audiobook chapters, voice clone playback

                STT (speech-to-text)Audio → textTranscription, voice commands

                Realtime Voice AgentsFull duplex conversationPhone support bot, live interview

              
              Your voice clone = TTS path with a specific voice ID. Not the same as a Voice Agent that listens on a phone line.

cards.title: Batch API (half-price homework)
cards.body:
Send 100 narration jobs or summaries async - processed within ~24h, 20-50% cheaper on tokens. Use for: processing long-form manuscript chapters overnight, bulk image prompts, dataset labeling.

              Tradeoff: not real-time. Wrong for Discord. Right for offline pipelines.

cards.title: Which API for which job
cards.body:
I need something to reply in Discord when I'm asleep

                  Grok 4.3 Chat API + a small always-on host (VPS, Railway, etc.). Your bot code receives Discord event → calls API → posts reply. Text only unless you add TTS upload.

                

                
                  I need to turn written stories into spoken audio

                  Voice API TTS. Chunk by chapter. Batch API if not urgent. Watch character count - 1M chars ≈ $15.

                

                
                  I need cover art or scene images for a fiction project

                  Imagine API (image). ~$0.02-0.05 per image. Script loops prompts → saves files.

                

                
                  I need help writing code in an IDE

                  Grok Build 0.1 via Grok Build / compatible tools. Already what you're doing. Separate from one-off personal app projects.

                

                
                  I need a phone number people can call and talk to AI

                  Voice Agents (realtime). Highest burn rate. Prototype in minutes, not hours, until you understand billing.

cards.title: Module 5 checklist
cards.body:
I understand tool calling = model triggers my code

                
I can distinguish TTS vs realtime voice agents

                
I picked which API matches my next actual project

cards.title: Troubleshooting
cards.body:
401 Unauthorized

                Key wrong, expired, or missing Bearer prefix. Regenerate in console. Re-export env var.

              

              
                Usage stays at $0 after "using" Grok Build

                IDE may use different billing or a bundled key. Check Grok Build settings for which API key is configured. Console only tracks requests made with your team key.

              

              
                402 / insufficient credits

                Balance empty. Add credits or enable top-up. Failed requests after this still may charge violation fee on some endpoints.

              

              
                429 Rate limit

                Too fast. Back off, retry with delay. Batch API for bulk work.

              

              
                Response empty or truncated

                Hit max_tokens cap. Increase cap or shorten input. Check finish_reason in response.

              

              
                App works locally, fails deployed

                Env var not set on server. Key committed to git and revoked. CORS only affects browser-direct calls - server-side bots don't have CORS issues.

              

              
                Credits dropping faster than expected

                Check console usage breakdown. Look for: long chat history resent every call, tool invocations, realtime voice left connected, image/video loops.

cards.title: One-pager (print this)
cards.body:
API in 30 seconds

                Program → HTTP POST + JSON + API key → xAI → JSON response → credits deducted.

                Your console

                
                  Credits = money left

                  Tokens = text units billed

                  Requests = call count

                

                Product picker

                
                  Text / bots → Grok 4.3 Chat API

                  Code / agents → Grok Build 0.1

                  Narration / clone → Voice TTS

                  Live phone voice → Voice Agents realtime

                  Pictures / video → Imagine API

                

                Cost guardrails

                
                  Learn with short text calls first

                  Cap max_tokens on bots

                  Batch for bulk offline work

                  Don't leave realtime voice running unattended

                

                First test command

                $env:XAI_API_KEY = "your-key"
# Then Invoke-RestMethod to https://api.x.ai/v1/responses
# See Module 3 card 4 in this app

                Links

                
                  Console: console.x.ai

                  Docs: docs.x.ai

                  Pricing: docs.x.ai/developers/pricing

cards.title: What to do next
cards.body:
You have theory. Pick one path:

              
                PathFirst actionAPI

                Prove the pipe worksRun Module 3 PowerShell callChat

                Automate textScript that reads a .txt file, sends chunks, saves repliesChat or Batch

                Voice outputOne TTS call on a single paragraph with your cloned voice IDVoice

                DiscordRegister bot in Discord Developer Portal, echo bot without AI first, then add API callChat

              
              Docs are reference. This app is the map. When you build, you'll look up endpoint specifics - you won't read them cover to cover.

              
                Bootcamp complete when

                Console shows >0 requests, you can explain request/response, you know which product fits your next build, and you know what would drain $50.
